event_date_text: Upcoming Events Past Events Upcoming Events 21 April 2026 - 23 April 2026 Conference Physical Event Localizing the SDGs Arab Forum for Sustainable Development The Arab Forum for Sustainable Development (AFSD) is the primary regional mechanism for the follow-up and review of the implementation of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development in the Arab region. AFSD brings together Arab Governments and a wide range of stakeholders to discuss progress, review national and regional experiences, and consider the latest developments and implementation mechanisms of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable… 21 April 2026 - 22 April 2026 Conference Physical Event Localizing the SDGs UNECE Regional Forum on Sustainable Development The Regional Forum on Sustainable Development was established by the Economic Commission for Europe at its sixty-seventh session in April 2017, with the aim to create “a regional mechanism to follow-up and review the implementation of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development” 22 April 2026 UN Day Global Event Climate Change International Mother Earth Day International Mother Earth Day aims to raise global public awareness of the challenges to the well-being of the planet and all the life it supports. The Day also recognizes a collective responsibility, as called for in the 1992 Rio Declaration, to promote harmony with nature and the Earth, to achieve a just balance among the economic, social, and environmental needs of the present and future generations of humanity. 28 April 2026 - 30 April 2026 Conference Physical Event Localizing the SDGs Twelfth Session of the Africa Regional Forum on Sustainable Development The Twelfth Session of the Africa Regional Forum on Sustainable Development is scheduled to take place from 28 to 30 April 2026. This session will focus on the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and will be a platform for assessing progress and exchanging knowledge, best practices, and policy solutions to support the implementation of the 2030 Agenda. 17 May 2026 - 22 May 2026 Conference Physical Event New Urban Agenda World Urban Forum (WUF13) WUF was established in 2001 by the United Nations to examine one of the most pressing issues facing the world today: rapid urbanization and its impact on communities, cities, economies, climate change and policies. The first WUF was held in Nairobi, Kenya in 2002 and has been held around the world ever since. Representatives of national, regional and local governments, academics, business people, community leaders, urban planners and… 22 May 2026 UN Day Global Event Climate Change International Day for Biological Diversity As the global community is called to re-examine our relationship to the natural world, one thing is certain: despite all our technological advances we are completely dependent on healthy and vibrant ecosystems for our water, food, medicines, clothes, fuel, shelter and energy. 5 June 2026 UN Day Physical Event Climate Change World Environment Day World Environment Day, which Baku will host on 5 June 2026, is the largest global platform for environmental engagement and action. Established by the UN General Assembly in 1972, the Day inspires and is celebrated by millions of people across the world, who participate in online and in-person activities and events around the world aimed at accelerating environmental progress for people and planet. 21 June 2026 - 24 June 2026 Conference Physical Event Localizing the SDGs Innovate4Cities Conference The 2026 Innovate4Cities Conference (I4C26) is the fourth global convening that brings together local, regional and national governments, academia, civil society, and industry to close priority knowledge gaps and provide an evidence base that can be used to deliver city climate action. 29 June 2026 - 30 June 2026 Partner Event Physical Event Localizing the SDGs Hamburg Sustainability Conference This year, the conference will focus on consolidating achievements and expanding its scope and outreach, recognizing the HSC’s unique role as a safe space for challenging conversations and unconventional exchanges. By addressing today’s most critical sustainability challenges, it aims to help re-envision a multilateralism that is fit for today’s world. It will bring together a diverse set of voices from different corners of the world to… 6 July 2026 - 15 July 2026 Conference Physical Event Localizing the SDGs High-level Political Forum on Sustainable Development (HLPF) The High-level Political Forum on Sustainable Development (HLPF) will be convened from Monday, 6 July, to Wednesday, 15 July 2026, under the auspices of the Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC). 11 July 2026 UN Day Global Event Human Rights World Population Day World Population Day is celebrated annually on 11 July to focus attention on the urgency and importance of population issues. 9 August 2026 UN Day Global Event Human Rights International Day of the World's Indigenous Peoples In order to raise awareness of the needs of these population groups, every 9 August commemorates the International Day of the World’s Indigenous Peoples, chosen in recognition of the first meeting of the UN Working Group on Indigenous Populations held in Geneva in 1982. 12 August 2026 UN Day Global Event Youth International Youth Day International Youth Day is commemorated every year on 12 August, bringing youth issues to the attention of the international community and celebrating the potential of youth as partners in today’s global society. The idea for International Youth Day was proposed in 1991 by the young people who were gathered in Vienna, Austria, for the first session of the World Youth Forum of the United Nations System. 19 August 2026 UN Day Global Event Human Rights World Humanitarian Day World Humanitarian Day is commemorated every year on 19 August to pay tribute to humanitarian workers killed and injured in the course of their work, and to honor all aid and health workers who continue to provide life-saving support and protection to people most in need. The Day highlights the importance, effectiveness, and positive impact of humanitarian work. 8 September 2026 - 22 September 2026 Conference Physical Event Localizing the SDGs 81st session of the UN General Assembly (UNGA 81) The 81st session of the UN General Assembly (UNGA 81) will open on Tuesday, 8 September 2026. The first day of the high-level General Debate will be Tuesday, 22 September 2026. It will be convened under the auspices of ECLAC and will be guided by the principles established for all follow-up and review processes by the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development, adopted in September 2015 by the United Nations General Assembly. It will involve… 8 April 2026 - 10 April 2026 Conference Physical Event Housing Second Africa Urban Forum (AUF2) The Second Africa Urban Forum (AUF2), convened by the African Union Commission and hosted by the Government of Kenya with technical support from UN-Habitat and UNECA, will be held in Nairobi, Kenya, from 8 to 10 April under the theme “Adequate Housing for All: Advancing Socio-economic and Environmental Transformation towards the realization of Agenda 2063.” Held ahead of the… 30 March 2026 Partner Event Global Event Climate Change International Day of Zero Waste The International Day of Zero Waste, observed annually on 30 March, highlights both the importance of bolstering waste management globally and the need to promote sustainable consumption and production patterns. 25 March 2026 - 27 March 2026 Conference Physical Event Climate Change World Sustainable Development Summit The World Sustainable Development Summit (WSDS) is the annual flagship multistakeholder convening organized by The Energy and Resources Institute (TERI). Instituted in 2001, the Summit series has a legacy of over two decades in making ‘sustainable development’ a globally shared goal. Over the years, the Summit platform has brought together thought leaders, heads of state and government, scholars, corporates, youth groups, and civil society… 22 March 2026 UN Day Global Event Water and Sanitation World Water Day World Water Day celebrates water and inspires action to tackle the global water crisis. A core focus of World Water Day is to support the achievement of Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 6: water and sanitation for all by 2030. 12 March 2026 Conference Physical Event New Urban Agenda PINUA (Partnership Implementing the New Urban Agenda) National Stakeholder Forum Are you ready to help shape inclusive and sustainable housing solutions in Kenya? Registration is now open for the PINUA (Partnership Implementing the New Urban Agenda) National Stakeholder Forum, taking place on 12 March 2026 at the Safari Park Hotel, Nairobi, Kenya, from 8am- 5pm. Kenya continues to face complex housing challenges, including affordability gaps, informal settlements and the need for climate-responsive… 9 March 2026 - 20 March 2026 Partner Event Physical Event Human Rights The seventieth session of the Commission on the Status of Women The seventieth session of the Commission on the Status of Women will take place at United Nations Headquarters in New York from 9 to 20 March 2026. Representatives of Member States, UN entities, and ECOSOC-accredited non-governmental organizations (NGOs) from all regions of the world are invited to attend the session 8 March 2026 UN Day Global Event Gender International Women's Day International Women’s Day is celebrated annually on 8 March. It offers an opportunity to reflect on progress made, to call for change, and to celebrate acts of courage and determination by ordinary women who have played an extraordinary role in the history of their countries and communities. 24 February 2026 - 27 February 2026 Conference Physical Event Localizing the SDGs Asia-Pacific Forum on Sustainable Development The Asia-Pacific Forum on Sustainable Development (APFSD) is an annual, inclusive intergovernmental forum to support follow-up and review of progress on the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development and the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) at the regional level. This regional platform aims to inform, empower, and support Asia-Pacific countries (especially least developed countries, landlocked developing countries and small island… 20 February 2026 UN Day Global Event Advocacy World Day of Social Justice Strengthening a just transition for a sustainable future means making sure our move toward low-carbon economies benefits everyone, especially the most vulnerable. It requires a holistic approach that weaves environmental sustainability with social justice, ensuring that workers, indigenous peoples, and marginalized communities receive the support they need through retraining, job creation, and strong social protection measures. 11 February 2026 - 15 February 2026 Conference Physical Event Capacity Building 39th African Union Summit The 39th AU Summit, scheduled for February 11-15, 2026, will be a significant event for African leaders. 11 February 2026 - 12 February 2026 Conference Physical Event Capacity Building 48th Ordinary Session of the Executive Council The 48th Ordinary Session of the Executive Council (ExC) of the African Union (AU). The Council is one of the African Union’s principal policy organs, composed of Ministers of Foreign Affairs or other designated Ministers of AU Member States. It is responsible for coordinating and taking decisions on policies in areas of common interest to Member States, and for preparing items for consideration by the Assembly of Heads of State and… 11 February 2026 - 12 February 2026 Partner Event Physical Event Housing Housing and Sustainable Urban Development Forum The Housing and Sustainable Urban Development Forum (HSUDF) will serve as a unique platform to drive the growth and innovation of the housing and urban development sector in Saudi Arabia. Bringing together industry leaders, experts, and decision-makers, the forum will provide updates on new regulatory requirements for the sector and ongoing projects. The forum is a must-attend for solution providers in the housing and urban development space… 3 February 2026 - 5 February 2026 Conference Physical Event Urban Governance World Governments Summit In 2026, the World Governments Summit will convene under the theme 'Shaping Future Governments.' This Summit will bring together governments, international organizations, thought leaders, and private sector leaders from around the globe to foster international cooperation and identify innovative solutions for future challenges, ultimately inspiring and empowering the next generation of governments. event_date_text: Shutterstock Shutterstock Calendar Get the latest on UNESCO activities worldwide Home All events Statutory meetings Informal meetings Cultural events Other events Jump to : Next international days Calls for applications and statutory deadlines This Month at UNESCO Global edition of the Master Class against Racism and Discriminations 1 April 2026 (UNESCO HQ - Paris, France) UNESCO is hosting the global edition of its Master Class Series against Racism and Discriminations, emphasizing the urgent need to address racial prejudice, discrimination, and intolerance. This Master Class presents a new phase of the UNESCO Master Class against Racism and Discriminations, putting youth action and real-world impact at the heart of the program. In this 2026 edition, we will focus on how to design and implement concrete activities in schools and communities as a result of young people’s participation. 224th Session of the Executive Board 8-23 April 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France) The Executive Board, one of the three constitutional organs of UNESCO, along with the General Conference and the Secretariat, is set to convene at UNESCO on the specified date. During this session, the Board, comprising of 58 Member States, will review the Organization's work program and budget estimates as submitted by the Director-General. UNESCO Roundtable “Remembering the 1994 genocide against the Tutsi: Education, memory and dialogue” 14 April 2026 (UNESCO HQ - Paris, France) UNESCO will mark this International Day by organizing a roundtable discussion on the role of education, memory and intergenerational dialogue in sustaining remembrance, promoting peace and preventing future atrocities. Paris Book Festival 17-19 April 2026 (Grand Palais, Paris) UNESCO will take part in this three-day book festival centered on the theme of “Travels”—exploring literary, interior, and geographical journeys, as well as migrations, exiles, and sensory experiences. Throughout the festival, visitors will enjoy exhibitions, talks, a dedicated bookshop area, and author signings. UNESCO will host a booth at the heart of the event, serving as a space for exchange, discovery, and engagement with its initiatives and publications. International Jazz Day 30 Apr 2026 (Chicago, The United States of America) To celebrate the power of jazz as a force for peace, dialogue and mutual understanding, UNESCO proclaimed 30 April as International Jazz Day in November 2011. This Day is an opportunity to foster greater appreciation – not only for music, but also for the contribution it can make to building more inclusive societies. This year a Global concert will take place in Chicago. This edition will mark the 15th anniversary of the event and coincide with the 250th anniversary of the United States of America. How to add "UNESCO Calendar of Events" to your mobile home screen? On Android: Open your browser and go to https://www.unesco.org/en/events Tap the three-dot menu icon (near the URL bar) Select “Add to Home screen” (Optional) Rename the shortcut if desired Tap “Add” to confirm On iPhone: Open Safari and navigate to https://www.unesco.org/en/events Tap the share icon (a square with an upward arrow) at the bottom Select “Add to Home Screen” (Optional) Rename the shortcut if desired Tap “Add” in the top-right corner What’s happening today? event_date_text: UNESCO Roundtable “Remembering the 1994 genocide against the Tutsi: Education, memory and dialogue” 14 April 2026 (UNESCO HQ - Paris, France) UNESCO will mark this International Day by organizing a roundtable discussion on the role of education, memory and intergenerational dialogue in sustaining remembrance, promoting peace and preventing future atrocities.

event_date_text: Paris Book Festival 17-19 April 2026 (Grand Palais, Paris) UNESCO will take part in this three-day book festival centered on the theme of “Travels”—exploring literary, interior, and geographical journeys, as well as migrations, exiles, and sensory experiences. Throughout the festival, visitors will enjoy exhibitions, talks, a dedicated bookshop area, and author signings. UNESCO will host a booth at the heart of the event, serving as a space for exchange, discovery, and engagement with its initiatives and publications.

event_date_text: International Jazz Day 30 Apr 2026 (Chicago, The United States of America) To celebrate the power of jazz as a force for peace, dialogue and mutual understanding, UNESCO proclaimed 30 April as International Jazz Day in November 2011. This Day is an opportunity to foster greater appreciation – not only for music, but also for the contribution it can make to building more inclusive societies. This year a Global concert will take place in Chicago. This edition will mark the 15th anniversary of the event and coincide with the 250th anniversary of the United States of America.

event_date_text: UNESCO UNESCO General Conference The General Conference is the highest governing body of UNESCO. Home President 43rd Session Documents Previous Sessions Secretariat The General Conference determines the policies and the main lines of work of the Organization Its duty is to set the programmes and the budget of UNESCO. It also elects the Members of the Executive Board and appoints, every four years, the Director-General. The working languages of the General Conference are Arabic, Chinese, English, French, Russian and Spanish. The General Conference consists of the representatives of UNESCO's Member States. It meets every two years, and is attended by Member States and Associate Members, together with observers for non-Member States, intergovernmental organizations and non-governmental organizations (NGOs). Each country has one vote, irrespective of its size or the extent of its contribution to the budget.
